description: Validates that promoted research-analyst subagent loads SKILL.md and applies the correct methodology. Tests anti-sycophancy protocol and source hierarchy from ds-analyze:research skill.
input_prompt: use research-analyst to analyze the TAM for enterprise AI coding assistants
expected_behavior: Claude invokes the research-analyst subagent which loads SKILL.md from ds-analyze:research. Response applies the structured methodology: states a research question, identifies assumptions, applies source hierarchy (Tier 1/2/3), searches for disconfirming evidence first, requires bottoms-up TAM reconciliation, and labels findings with confidence levels.
negative_checks: Claude does not give a single TAM number without sourcing or bottoms-up reconciliation
Claude does not skip the disconfirming evidence step
Claude does not present vendor market research as ground truth without triangulation
fixture_transcript:
User: use research-analyst to analyze the TAM for enterprise AI coding assistants

Claude: [Loaded: ~/.claude/skills/ds-analyze/modes/research/SKILL.md]

## Research Question
What is the addressable market for enterprise AI coding assistants (products like GitHub Copilot, Cursor, etc.) targeting software development teams at companies with 100+ engineers?

## Assumption Inventory
- [unverified] Enterprise buyers will pay $20-50/dev/month — needs WTP evidence
- [unverified] Adoption rate among eligible companies will reach 60%+ — needs survey data
- [structural] TAM depends on definition of 'enterprise' (100+ vs 1000+ engineers)

## Disconfirming Evidence (searched first)
Strongest disconfirm: GitHub Copilot Enterprise reports suggest many pilots fail to expand due to security/compliance friction. If regulatory blockers are widespread, TAM is lower than developer count implies.

## TAM Estimates (preliminary — triangulation incomplete)
Top-down: Analyst reports suggest $10-25B by 2027 [Tier 2, single source — requires triangulation]
Bottoms-up: ~500k companies with 100+ devs x ~15% adoption x 10 devs/company x $400/dev/yr = ~$300M initial SAM
Reconciliation gap: >10x — investigate before using either figure.

## Confidence: LOW
Requires 3 independent primary sources and bottoms-up reconciliation before findings are actionable.
